category_title: Fourth-grade students with behavioral challenges and poor work completion despite adequate literacy skills
scenario_title: Small group intervention pull-out during homeroom homework time
minimal_context: The student is participating in a small group pull-out session and is asked to write a personal memory goal on their goal card.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's take out our goal cards. Today we are learning about Strengthening Your Memory, so please write down a goal to remember one fact learned in class tomorrow.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't really need to write a goal for that. I'm already really competent at remembering things. I'd give myself a 90% on that already.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you think you could write down? Just try to put one goal on your card.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Pushes the goal card away) I have a lot of class assignments and homework I haven't finished yet. I should be doing that.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We need to finish this activity for our intervention block. Keep trying.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I'm not enjoying being in this small group. Everyone else in homeroom is using this time to work on their homework. I don't like that I 'missed intervention' in the classroom. If you ask me what I like about being pulled out here, I'd say 'Nothing except leaving.'

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's take out our goal cards. Today we are learning about Strengthening Your Memory, so please write down a goal to remember one fact learned in class tomorrow.

STUDENT: I don't really need to write a goal for that. I'm already really competent at remembering things. I'd give myself a 90% on that already.

TUTOR: What do you think you could write down? Just try to put one goal on your card.

STUDENT: (Pushes the goal card away) I have a lot of class assignments and homework I haven't finished yet. I should be doing that.

TUTOR: We need to finish this activity for our intervention block. Keep trying.

STUDENT: I'm not enjoying being in this small group. Everyone else in homeroom is using this time to work on their homework. I don't like that I 'missed intervention' in the classroom. If you ask me what I like about being pulled out here, I'd say 'Nothing except leaving.'

task.content: Participating in a character development intervention focused on improving goal setting, decision making, and self-management skills, or a reading practice activity using 'The Tale of Despereaux'
task.learning_goal: Improving self-regulation skills, engagement, and motivation to meet behavioral and instructional needs

context.environment: Small group setting during a 30 min intervention block built into the regular school day
context.materials: Goal cards, school-wide reinforcement tickets, 'The Tale of Despereaux' book
context.social_setting: Small group instruction pulling students out of their homeroom classes
context.time_context: During a 30-minute intervention block when other students in homeroom classes are working on homework and classwork

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the student realizes other students in their homeroom classes are using the time to work on homework and classwork during the intervention block
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Made informal comments to the instructor indicating they were dissatisfied, stated they did not like that they missed intervention in the classroom, and indicated they were not enjoying being in the small group
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Dissatisfied; feeling they are missing other experiences viewed as preferable or highly valued

behavior_patterns.condition: When asked to evaluate their own skills or need for improvement prior to intervention
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Rated their pre-intervention scores quite high, close to 90%, indicating they perceived little room for improvement
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Positive illusory bias meaning they tend to see themselves as more competent than do their teachers, parents, and peers

behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Exhibits limited work completion, off-task behavior, and difficulty completing assignments according to teacher-maintained records
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Low academic engagement, limited motivation, and limited self-determined behaviors
